title: My Best Dividend Aristocrats For June 2023
text: Dividend aristocrats are struggling in 2023, with the ProShares S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats ETF down 4.71% in May. Three strategies for identifying winning aristocrats include focusing on undervalued stocks, fastest expected growth, and a blend of the two. Despite recent underperformance, long-term investors can find value in dividend aristocrats, as they have historically outperformed the S&P 500 index.

title: Billionaire Investor Says 'Sell Real Estate'
text: Billionaire Charlie Munger recently warned investors. He said that commercial real estate is facing severe challenges.

title: AvalonBay Vs. Essex Property: What's The Best REIT To Buy Today?
text: It is arguably the best time since 2008 to buy apartment REITs. AvalonBay Communities and Essex Property Trust are both heavily discounted.

title: Buy The Dip: 2 REITs Getting Way Too Cheap
text: There are some unbelievable bargains in the REIT sector. Their cash flows have kept on rising even as their share prices collapsed.

title: REITs Will Go Bankrupt? Not So Fast
text: REITs are facing an "existential crisis" according to some investors. They claim that REITs will go bankrupt as they fail to refinance their debt.

title: Getting Ready For A 'Higher-For-Longer' Scenario For REITs
text: In the past decade, many REITs have become complacent and devised aggressive financing strategies that were underpinned by low interest rates. The sudden shift in the interest rates and a probability of a "higher-for-longer" scenario can put a notable share of REITs in a "nuclear winter" territory in my view.

title: Essex Property Trust: Things Are Just As Good As I Expected
text: Essex Property Trust, Inc. reported Q1 2023 earnings on Friday last week - I am exposed to both AvalonBay Communities, Inc. and Essex as some of my major apartment REITs, and expecting great things out of both. People have been worried that interest rate and macro trends will turn things down.

title: Essex Property Trust Declares Quarterly Distributions
text: SAN MATEO, Calif--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE:ESS) announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $2.31 per common share, payable July 14, 2023 to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2023. About Essex Property Trust, Inc. title: The Beauty Of REIT Dividend Aristocrats
text: A portfolio exposure to dividend aristocrats is extremely topical in periods where the economic dynamics are volatile and imply an elevated probability of distress. The problem with dividend aristocrat ETFs or just a pure-play replication of the entire universe of dividend aristocrats is that it provides an unattractive yield (~1.9%).

title: Rental Properties Vs. Residential REITs: Which Is Better?
text: Rentals have much more leverage earlier on, which means beginners can earn higher returns. REITs have lower variance of returns due to diversification and lower leverage. They also have better economies of scale, which results in better operating margin.

title: Buy Alert: 2 Top Dividend Growth Stocks With Secular Tailwinds
text: The stock market could go up or down. But a few years from now, the best quality stocks will be worth more.
